steps.explanation: The function $f(x) = 9{,}000(1.03)^x$ gives the value of the CD in dollars, where $x$ is the number of years after the investment.
Plugging in $x = 2$ gives the value 2 years after the investment: $f(2) = 9{,}000(1.03)^2 \approx 9{,}548.10$.
So $f(2) \approx 9{,}548.10$ means the total value of the CD (not the change from the initial amount) is approximately 9,548.10 dollars at 2 years.

• A: Incorrect — the function uses 3% interest, and $x$ is years, not a rate.
  • B: Incorrect — $x = 2$ is years, not days, and $f(2)$ is not 2 times the initial value.
  • C: 2 years after the investment, the value of the CD was approximately 9,548.10 dollars. This correctly interprets $x = 2$ as years and $f(2)$ as total value. That confirms C is correct.
  • D: Incorrect — 9,548.10 dollars is the total value, not the amount above the initial 9,000 dollars.
